## CRITICAL GOVERNANCE RULE
**ANALYSIS AND DOCUMENTATION ONLY until Mainframe Gate is approved (APEX Week 17).**
Claude MUST NOT generate COBOL code, JCL, or any artifact intended for direct deployment
to the mainframe without explicit human review and APEX Governance Board sign-off.
See: `../../governance/policies/mainframe-gate.md`

## Platform
- OS: IBM z/OS [version]
- COBOL: IBM Enterprise COBOL 6.x
- CICS: [version] (for online transactions)
- DB2: z/OS [version]
- JCL: [describe job scheduling tool — TWS/OPC, CA7, etc.]
- Data: VSAM KSDS/ESDS, QSAM sequential files, DB2 tables
- MQ: IBM MQ [version] (integration channel to Java services)

## IBM i (AS/400) — if applicable
- Language: RPG IV / RPGLE (ILE), CL
- Database: DB2 for i
- DDS: Physical files, logical files, display files

## What Claude CAN do in this repo (analysis only)
- Explain what a COBOL program does in plain English
- Identify external CALL statements and copybook dependencies
- Document business rules embedded in procedural logic
- Map WORKING-STORAGE to equivalent Java/Spring data structures
- Assess migration readiness (complexity, external dependencies, risk)
- Generate runbook documentation for undocumented programs
- Decode JCL to explain job flow and step dependencies
- Identify dead code, unreferenced paragraphs, unused fields

## Useful APEX prompts for this repo
```
"Explain what [PROGRAM].cbl does in plain English.
 List: purpose, inputs (datasets/queues), outputs, CALL dependencies,
 CICS commands used, and error handling strategy."

"Generate a runbook for [PROGRAM].cbl including:
 run prerequisites, input dataset formats, output datasets,
 restart/recovery procedure, and common abend codes."

"Identify the business rules in the [SECTION] section of [PROGRAM].cbl
 and show the equivalent Java/Spring logic structure."

"Map the WORKING-STORAGE fields in [PROGRAM].cbl to a Java record/DTO.
 Note any date format conversions needed (PIC 9(8) YYYYMMDD → LocalDate)."

"Assess the migration complexity of [PROGRAM].cbl.
 Rate: Low/Medium/High. Identify blockers for Java rewrite."
```

## Modernisation track (APEX Phase 6 — Week 17+)
- Agent: `ibmi-modernization-expert` from eeik_bootstrap
- Pattern: Strangler Fig — expose COBOL logic via REST/MQ, replace incrementally
- Integration bridge: Spring Boot → IBM MQ → COBOL program (interim state)
- Target: Spring Batch equivalent for batch programs; Spring Boot for online

## Copybook library
- Location: [HLQ.COPYLIB] / `copybooks/` in this repo (version-controlled copies)
- Standard copybooks: [list shared copybooks and their purpose]

## What Claude must NOT do
- Do NOT generate deployable COBOL, JCL, or CL programs (analysis phase)
- Do NOT suggest changes to production datasets
- Do NOT recommend CICS commands without CICS specialist review
- Do NOT assume field lengths without checking the actual copybook
- Do NOT use modern COBOL syntax if the target is COBOL 4.x or below
